data.local_name_citation: Bulletin of Department of Plant Resources No. 37 (2007) and Journal of Plant Resources Vol. 12 (2014), Department of Plant Resources, Nepal.
data.growth_form: Perennial herb or shrub, 30–100 cm tall, with erect inconspicuously ribbed villose stems.
data.location_found_nepal: Surkhet (West Nepal), Parsa Wildlife Reserve (Central Nepal); native range includes Bhutan, Nepal, Thailand, Vietnam, and India.
data.location_found_nepal_citation: eFlora of India (Acilepis squarrosa) and Bulletin of Department of Plant Resources No. 37 (2007).
data.traditional_usage: Used for treatment of diarrhea, dysentery, kidney stones, back pain, ulcers, wounds, luxation, dropsy, and as a scaricidal agent (flower head). Also utilized in Traditional Chinese Medicine for infections and dermatitis.
data.traditional_usage_citation: Das et al. (2021), Plant Science Today 8(2) and Das et al. (2024), Pharmacological Research – Modern Chinese Medicine.
data.preparation_solvent: Chloroform, Methanol, 80% Methanol (Hydro-methanolic), Petroleum ether, Ethyl acetate.
data.preparation_method: Shade-drying of leaves at room temperature followed by grinding into fine powder; sequential extraction in HPLC-grade organic solvents (petroleum ether, chloroform, ethyl acetate, methanol) using a Soxhlet apparatus at 60 °C for 48–90 hours.
data.preparation_method_citation: Das et al. (2024), Pharmacological Research – Modern Chinese Medicine.
data.extraction_solvent: Chloroform (identified as the most active for antibacterial properties), 80% Methanol.
data.compound_class: Phenols, flavonoids, coumarins, tannins, terpenoids, steroids, cardiac glycosides, alkaloids, and saponins.
data.compound_class_citation: Das et al. (2024), Pharmacological Research – Modern Chinese Medicine.
data.parts_with_amr: Leaves (most active), flower head, fruits and seeds.
data.parts_with_amr_citation: Das et al. (2021) and Das et al. (2024).

pathogen_data.preparation_method: Soxhlet extraction of shade-dried leaf powder
